text: For building and maintaining muscle mass, an overall daily protein intake of 1.4-2.0 g/kg body weight/day is sufficient for most exercising individuals.
Higher protein intakes of 2.3-3.1 g/kg/day may be needed to maximize retention of lean body mass during hypocaloric (calorie-deficit) periods.
Optimal protein intake per serving to maximize muscle protein synthesis is approximately 0.25 g/kg body weight, or an absolute dose of 20-40 g.
Acute protein doses should contain 700-3000 mg of leucine alongside a balanced array of essential amino acids to optimally stimulate muscle protein synthesis.
Protein doses should be evenly distributed every 3-4 hours across the day for optimal muscle protein synthesis.
The anabolic effect of exercise on muscle protein synthesis is long-lasting (at least 24 hours) but likely diminishes with increasing time post-exercise.
Pre-sleep casein protein intake of 30-40 g increases overnight muscle protein synthesis and metabolic rate without influencing lipolysis.
Endurance athletes should prioritize adequate carbohydrate intake for optimal performance, with added protein to help offset muscle damage and promote recovery.
